description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged and weathered surface, featuring a mix of jagged and smoother areas. The coloration is predominantly a pale gray with subtle streaks of white and hints of beige, suggesting mineral layering or sedimentary origins. The rock has a somewhat blocky form with one side appearing more angular and the opposite side more rounded and eroded. Fine striations and natural grooves run across its surface, giving it a textured, tactile appearance. The overall mass and density suggest it is composed primarily of stone, likely limestone or a similar sedimentary rock. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A pale gray, irregularly shaped rock with rugged, striated surfaces and a mix of angular and rounded edges. Its weathered appearance and substantial size make it suitable as a natural barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest face, with prominent striations and a slightly sloped top surface.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears more angular, with a pronounced ridge and a visible crevice along one side.
description_view_2: This side highlights the rock's elongated form and a rough, uneven surface with subtle color variations.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals a flatter, more eroded surface with gentle curves and less pronounced ridges.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
